# Style Reference Guide

<!-- Generated by design-style-agent -->
<!-- MortgageMax Commission Payments POC — BetterBond-Commission-Payments-POC-002 -->

## Brand Overview

**MortgageMax** is a South African mortgage origination company. This internal commission payments tool is used exclusively by BetterBond payments administrators (admin role) and read-only real estate agency staff (viewer role). The application must convey trust, financial precision, and institutional authority.

**Brand palette:**
- Deep navy `#0D2D5E` — primary brand colour; used for key UI elements in light mode and as the dark-mode background base
- Light teal `#7EC8E3` — accent; used decoratively and as the primary interactive colour in dark mode
- White `#FFFFFF` — clean, open backgrounds in light mode

**Critical constraint:** Teal (`#7EC8E3`) on a white or light background fails WCAG AA contrast (1.87:1). Never render teal as text against a white/light background. Teal is safe as: chart fill, icon strokes/decorative elements on white, borders, and text/icons on navy or dark navy backgrounds.

---

## Typography

### Font Family

Use the existing Geist Sans (set via `--font-sans: var(--font-geist-sans)` in the `@theme inline` block). Geist is clean and modern — suitable for dense data grids and professional UI. No custom fonts need to be loaded.

### Heading Hierarchy

| Level | Usage | Tailwind classes |
|-------|-------|-----------------|
| `<h1>` | Page title (Dashboard, Payment Management, etc.) — one per page | `text-2xl font-semibold text-primary` |
| `<h2>` | Section headers within a page (e.g., "Agency Summary", "Parked Payments") | `text-lg font-semibold text-primary` |
| `<h3>` | Sub-section or card headers | `text-base font-medium text-primary` |
| `<p>` body | Table cell content, descriptive text | `text-sm text-foreground` |
| Caption / label | Form labels, column headers, metadata | `text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide` |

### Data Grid Typography

- Column headers: `text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide`
- Cell content: `text-sm text-foreground`
- Currency values: `text-sm font-medium text-foreground tabular-nums` — always use `tabular-nums` for alignment in currency columns
- Status badges: `text-xs font-medium` in a `<Badge>` component (see Component Selection below)

### Currency Formatting

All currency values must be formatted using the South African locale (`en-ZA`):

```typescript
const formatCurrency = (value: number): string =>
  new Intl.NumberFormat('en-ZA', {
    style: 'currency',
    currency: 'ZAR',
    minimumFractionDigits: 2,
  }).format(value);
// Output: R 1 234 567,89
```

Note: `Intl.NumberFormat` with `en-ZA` + `ZAR` produces the `R` prefix with space as thousands separator and comma as decimal — matching the required `R 1 234 567,89` format.

---

## Spacing

Use Tailwind's default spacing scale. The following conventions apply throughout:

| Context | Rule | Tailwind |
|---------|------|----------|
| Page outer padding | Consistent horizontal page padding | `px-6 py-6` (desktop), `px-4 py-4` (mobile) |
| Card internal padding | Inside `<Card>` components | `p-6` for content cards, `p-4` for compact metric cards |
| Section gap | Vertical space between page sections | `space-y-6` or `gap-6` in grid layouts |
| Form field spacing | Between label and input, between fields | `space-y-2` within a field group, `space-y-4` between fields |
| Grid gap | Between grid columns and rows | `gap-4` for dashboard metric cards, `gap-6` for section-level grids |
| Modal padding | Internal modal content | `p-6` |
| Button group gap | Between related buttons (e.g., Park / Park Selected) | `gap-2` |
| Sidebar nav item | Padding within a nav link | `px-3 py-2` |

Dashboard metric cards sit in a 2-column grid on tablet and 4-column grid on desktop. Keep card heights consistent by using uniform padding rather than min-height where possible.

---

## Component Selection

### Cards (`<Card>`)

Use `<Card>` from Shadcn for:
- Dashboard metric tiles (Total Value Ready for Payment, Total Value Parked, etc.)
- The Agency Summary section wrapper
- Parked Payments Aging Report wrapper
- Any discrete data grouping that needs visual separation

Do NOT use `<Card>` for:
- The main data grids (Payment Management, Payments Made) — use a bare `<div>` with `border border-border rounded-lg overflow-hidden` to frame the table
- Navigation containers

### Dialogs (`<Dialog>`)

Use `<Dialog>` from Shadcn for all confirmation modals:
- Park single payment confirmation
- Park Selected confirmation
- Unpark / Unpark Selected confirmation
- Initiate Payment confirmation
- Reset Demo confirmation

All confirmation modals follow this structure:
```
<Dialog>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Are you sure you want to park this payment?</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>Agent Name · Claim Date · Amount</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter>
      <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
      <Button variant="default">Confirm</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
```

Destructive actions (Reset Demo) use `<Button variant="destructive">` on the confirm button.

### Tables

Use a plain HTML `<table>` with Tailwind utility classes rather than a Shadcn data table abstraction. The grids have complex behaviour (multi-select, conditional row actions, pagination). Style pattern:

```
<table className="w-full text-sm">
  <thead className="bg-muted text-muted-foreground">
    <tr>
      <th className="px-4 py-3 text-left text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wide">...</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody className="divide-y divide-border">
    <tr className="hover:bg-muted/50 transition-colors">...</tr>
  </tbody>
</table>
```

On mobile (< 768px), data grids collapse to card-per-row layout. Each card shows the most important fields; secondary fields are hidden or placed behind an expand toggle.

### Badges (Status)

Use `<Badge>` from Shadcn for payment Status values (REG, MAN-PAY):
- `REG`: `<Badge variant="outline">REG</Badge>` in navy border
- `MAN-PAY`: `<Badge variant="secondary">MAN-PAY</Badge>`

### Toast Notifications

Use `<Sonner>` (the Shadcn toast provider, from `sonner` package) for all API feedback:
- Success toasts: bottom-right, auto-dismiss after 4 seconds
- Error toasts: bottom-right, dismissible via "Dismiss" button, do NOT auto-dismiss — user must acknowledge errors

Toast position: `position="bottom-right"` on the `<Toaster>` component.

### Select / Dropdown for Agency Selector

Use `<Select>` from Shadcn for the agency selector in Payment Management when no agency query param is present. The agency list is sourced from the dashboard `PaymentsByAgency` array.

### Theme Toggle

Use a `<Button variant="ghost" size="icon">` with a sun/moon icon (from `lucide-react`) for the theme switcher in the top navigation bar. Toggle between light and dark classes on `<html>` and persist in `localStorage`.

### Skeleton / Loading States

Use `<Skeleton>` from Shadcn during data fetch operations. Show skeleton rows matching the expected grid layout rather than a full-page spinner, except for the initial page load where a centred spinner is acceptable.

```tsx
// Grid loading state pattern
<div className="space-y-2">
  {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, i) => (
    <Skeleton key={i} className="h-10 w-full" />
  ))}
</div>
```

---

## Layout

### Top Navigation Bar

The nav bar is fixed at the top (`sticky top-0 z-50`) with a navy background in light mode (`bg-primary`) and a darker navy surface in dark mode. Height: `h-16`.

Navigation links:
- Inactive: `text-primary-foreground/70 hover:text-primary-foreground`
- Active: `text-primary-foreground font-medium underline-offset-4 underline` or a bottom-border indicator

The MortgageMax logo sits on the far left. The theme toggle sits on the far right, followed by a sign-out button.

**Role-based nav links (left to right):**
- Dashboard (all roles)
- Payment Management (admin only — conditionally rendered)
- Payments Made (all roles)
- Users (admin only — conditionally rendered)

### Page Content Width

Max content width: `max-w-7xl mx-auto` (1280px). Apply this wrapper to all page content inside the nav.

### Dashboard Grid

```
Desktop (1280px+): 4-column metric row, then full-width charts, then full-width Agency Summary grid
Tablet (768px–1279px): 2-column metric row, full-width charts, full-width grid
Mobile (<768px): Single-column metric cards, charts become horizontally scrollable, grid becomes card list
```

Dashboard metric cards layout:
```
grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 xl:grid-cols-4 gap-4
```

### Sidebar

The sidebar (`<sidebar>` component) is navy-coloured (`bg-sidebar` = `#0D2D5E` in light, `#0F1E35` in dark). It is used only if a sidebar navigation layout is adopted; the FRS describes a top navigation bar. If implementing a sidebar layout, the sidebar is always expanded on desktop and collapsible on mobile.

### Payment Management Layout

Two-panel vertical layout:
1. Top: Search/filter bar + "Park Selected" and "Initiate Payment" action buttons
2. Middle: Main Grid (scrollable, full width)
3. Bottom: Parked Grid (collapsible section header, scrollable)

### Responsive Behaviour

| Breakpoint | Pattern |
|-----------|---------|
| `<768px` mobile | Data grids become card lists. Filter/search controls open as `<Sheet>` (bottom drawer) via `<Sheet>` Shadcn component. Action buttons collapse into a `<DropdownMenu>`. |
| `768px–1279px` tablet | Grids show reduced column set (omit lower-priority columns like Grant Date, Reg Date). |
| `1280px+` desktop | Full column set visible. |

---

## Motion and Animation

The project already includes `tw-animate-css` and custom keyframe animations in `globals.css` (`modalEnter`, `modalExit`, `slideInFromRight`, `slideOutToRight`).

### Guidelines

- **Modals**: Use `modalEnter` / `modalExit` keyframes for dialog open/close. Duration: 150ms ease-out.
- **Toasts**: Use `slideInFromRight` for toast entry. Duration: 200ms ease-out.
- **Row interactions**: Table row hover transitions: `transition-colors duration-100`.
- **Theme toggle**: No animation on theme switch — apply instantly (avoids flash of transitioning colours during page load).
- **Chart animations**: Recharts (if used) has built-in animation. Keep it enabled at the default duration (1500ms) for dashboard charts — it conveys that data has loaded. Disable for subsequent filter updates (pass `isAnimationActive={false}` once loaded).
- **Loading skeletons**: Use a subtle pulse animation (Shadcn Skeleton default) rather than a spinner for grid loading states.

### Reduced Motion

The `globals.css` already contains `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` that collapses all animation durations to 0.01ms. No additional work needed — do not override this media query.

---

## Accessibility

### Contrast Requirements (WCAG 2.1 AA)

| Pair | Ratio | Mode |
|------|-------|------|
| White text on navy primary `#0D2D5E` | 13.51:1 | Light |
| Navy text on white background | 13.51:1 | Light |
| Muted grey text `#5A6B82` on white | 5.44:1 | Light |
| Near-white text `#E2ECF6` on dark bg `#0D1F3C` | 13.74:1 | Dark |
| Teal `#7EC8E3` on dark bg `#0D1F3C` | 8.81:1 | Dark |
| Teal `#7EC8E3` on dark card `#152844` | 7.93:1 | Dark |
| Navy `#0D2D5E` on teal `#7EC8E3` | 7.24:1 | Both |

**Teal prohibition:** Never use `text-accent` or any teal colour as body text or link text against a white/light-grey background. This includes disabled/placeholder states. The combination produces 1.87:1 — a hard WCAG AA failure.

### Focus Rings

All interactive elements must show the `--ring` token as a visible focus outline:
- Light mode: navy focus ring (`--ring: oklch(0.305 0.095 259.1)`)
- Dark mode: teal focus ring (`--ring: oklch(0.794 0.083 223.3)`)

Use Shadcn's default focus-ring behaviour (`outline-ring/50` applied via `@layer base * {}`). Do NOT suppress focus outlines on any element, including table rows with checkbox selection.

### Keyboard Navigation

- All confirmation modals must trap focus (Shadcn `<Dialog>` handles this automatically)
- The data grid must support `Tab` through row checkboxes and action buttons
- The "Park", "Unpark" buttons in grid rows must be focusable and activatable with `Enter`/`Space`
- The theme toggle button must have `aria-label="Toggle theme"` and `aria-pressed` reflecting the current state

### Screen Reader Considerations

- Status badges must have meaningful text content — `REG` and `MAN-PAY` are sufficient as-is
- Chart components must include `aria-label` on the chart container and a visually-hidden data summary table for screen reader users
- Loading states must use `aria-live="polite"` or `aria-busy="true"` on the content region
- Empty state messages use `role="status"` on the containing element
- The `LastChangedUser` audit header is sent silently — no user-visible indication needed

### ARIA for Data Grids

```tsx
<table aria-label="Payment Management — Main Grid" aria-busy={isLoading}>
  <caption className="sr-only">
    Payments for {agencyName} — {payments.length} records
  </caption>
  ...
</table>
```

---

## Brand Tone

### Microcopy Style

This is a professional internal financial tool. Use formal, concise language. Avoid casual contractions in system messages. Use second person ("you") only when necessary.

### Action Labels (Buttons)

| Action | Button label |
|--------|-------------|
| Park a payment | "Park" |
| Park multiple | "Park Selected ({n})" |
| Unpark | "Unpark" |
| Unpark multiple | "Unpark Selected ({n})" |
| Initiate payment batch | "Initiate Payment" |
| Confirm a modal action | "Confirm" |
| Cancel a modal | "Cancel" |
| Download invoice PDF | "Download Invoice" |
| Reset demo data | "Reset Demo" |
| Sign out | "Sign Out" |
| Toggle theme | (icon only, with `aria-label="Toggle theme"`) |

### Confirmation Modal Copy

All confirmation modals follow this pattern — formal, precise, states the consequence:

- Park: "Are you sure you want to park this payment? This will remove it from the current payment run."
- Bulk park: "Are you sure you want to park {n} payments? These will be removed from the current payment run. Total value: R {amount}."
- Initiate Payment: "Initiate payment batch for {agencyName}? This will create a new payment batch for {n} payments totalling R {amount}. This action cannot be undone."
- Reset Demo: "Are you sure you want to reset the demo data? This will permanently delete all current data and restore the sample dataset."

### Error Messages

Error toast messages should relay the API error message directly (which will be in English). Prefix with context:

- "Failed to park payment: {api error message}"
- "Failed to initiate payment: {api error message}"
- "Failed to load payments: {api error message}"

Avoid vague messages like "Something went wrong." If the API provides no error detail, use "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again."

### Empty State Messages

| Screen | Empty state heading | Subtitle |
|--------|--------------------|---------| 
| Payment Management — Main Grid | "No payments available for {Agency Name}" | "All payments for this agency have been parked or no payments are ready for processing." |
| Payment Management — Parked Grid | "No parked payments" | "Park payments from the main grid above to hold them from the current payment run." |
| Payments Made | "No payment batches found" | "Completed payment batches will appear here once payments have been initiated." |

### Date Formatting

Display dates in `dd MMM yyyy` format (e.g., `12 Mar 2025`) — consistent with South African business convention. Use `date-fns` with `format(date, 'dd MMM yyyy')`.

### Number Formatting

- Currency: `R 1 234 567,89` — use `Intl.NumberFormat('en-ZA', { style: 'currency', currency: 'ZAR' })`
- Commission percentage: `0.95%` — always 2 decimal places, `toFixed(2)` + `%` suffix
- Payment counts: plain integer, no formatting (e.g., `42 payments`)

---

## Chart Guidelines (Dashboard)

The dashboard uses bar charts and an aging report chart (Recharts is the recommended library for this Next.js/React setup).

### Colour Assignment

Bar charts use the `--chart-*` tokens:
- Primary data series (Bond Comm / Ready for Payment): `var(--chart-1)` — navy in light, teal in dark
- Secondary data series (Manual Payments / Parked): `var(--chart-2)` — teal in light, teal-light in dark
- Aging report ranges (1–3d, 4–7d, >7d): `var(--chart-1)`, `var(--chart-3)`, `var(--chart-4)` for visual distinction

### Chart Accessibility

Each chart must have:
- `aria-label` on the `<ResponsiveContainer>` wrapper
- A `<title>` element inside the SVG (Recharts exposes this via the `<CartesianGrid>` or custom wrapper)
- A visually-hidden `<table>` summary of the chart data for screen readers

### Tooltip Style

Recharts tooltips: use `contentStyle={{ backgroundColor: 'var(--card)', border: '1px solid var(--border)', borderRadius: 'var(--radius)', color: 'var(--card-foreground)' }}` to match the design system.

---

## Logo Usage

The MortgageMax logo (`documentation/morgagemaxlogo.png`) should be placed in `web/public/morgagemaxlogo.png` and referenced as `<Image src="/morgagemaxlogo.png" alt="MortgageMax" width={180} height={40} />` in the navigation bar.

In dark mode, the logo (navy text on transparent/white background) may not be legible. Options:
1. Use `className="invert"` on the `<Image>` in dark mode — this inverts the navy to near-white
2. Request a white/light variant of the logo from design

For the POC, apply `dark:invert` on the logo image as a pragmatic solution.
